title: Fetal Growth Restriction
authors: Devaskar SU, Janzen C, Calkins KL / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Fetal Growth Restriction?
FGR คือ leading cause of perinatal morbidity/mortality รองจาก prematurity เท่านั้น — ~10% of live-born infants; 23% attributable risk for stillbirth (Flenady Lancet 2011). สิ่งที่ต้องรู้: (1) SGA ≠ FGR — most SGA constitutionally small; communication ob-neonatology ต้องชัดเจน; (2) AEDV/REDV prenatal → risk NEC 4x — เตรียม trophic feeds HM ทันทีหลังคลอด; (3) 70% SGA มี postnatal hypoglycemia — glucose infusion rate 6–8 mg/kg/min > AGA; (4) FGR SGA 24–26 wk → 2x risk CLD; ROP worse staging กว่า AGA; (5) DOHaD — fetal adaptation → adult obesity/T2DM/CVD/HTN/NAFLD — เราเป็น physician คนแรกที่ set trajectory ชีวิตนี้.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย FGR vs SGA: definition, terminology, ทำไม false-positive rate สูงเมื่อใช้ SGA เป็น proxy
เปรียบเทียบ symmetric vs asymmetric FGR: สัดส่วน, etiology, cell size vs number, head-sparing pattern
ระบุ etiologies ทั้ง 3 กลุ่ม: fetal/maternal/placental — พร้อม specific examples จาก Chapter 14
อธิบาย pathophysiology: incomplete spiral artery remodeling, insulin/catecholamine/IGF axis
วิเคราะห์ antenatal screening: fundal height specificity/NPV, US serial every 3–4 wk, AC stand-alone NPV, UA Doppler benefit
ระบุ delivery timing: ACOG recommendations ตาม isolated vs complicated FGR; DIGITAT/GRIT trial key findings
จัดการ postnatal hypoglycemia: GIR target, ทำไม FGR ต้องการ GIR สูงกว่า AGA, diazoxide indication
อธิบาย respiratory complications: RDS/PPHN/CLD — mechanism; SGA vs AGA preterm CLD risk
ระบุ NEC risk ใน FGR + AEDV/REDV: mechanism, 4x risk, feeding strategy (trophic + HM), PNALD
เชื่อม DOHaD: metabolic programming, catch-up growth trade-off, cardiometabolic sequelae ใน adult
1. Definitions and Terminology

FGR ≠ SGA — ความต่างที่สำคัญทางคลินิก
FGR: fetus fails to reach in utero growth potential due to pathologic insult — may be born with BW <10th or even >10th percentile. SGA: birth weight <10th percentile for GA — most are constitutionally small (false-positive rate สูง). AGA: BW 10th–90th percentile. LGA: BW >90th percentile. "Restriction" preferred over "retardation" (ตามทั้ง SMFM 2020 และ Fanaroff 2025). ดังนั้น: communication ระหว่าง OB และ neonatologist ต้องระบุว่า SGA infant รายนี้มี true in utero growth deceleration จริงหรือไม่ — เพราะ management ต่างกันมาก.
Incidence: ~10% live-born infants; substantially higher among stillborns
Stillbirth risk: EFW <10th perc → 1.5% risk (2x normal); EFW <5th perc → 2.5% risk
Meta-analysis 96 population studies: SGA attributable risk for stillbirth ~23% (Flenady Lancet 2011;377:1331)
40% FGR cases undetected — stillbirths occur even with routine US screening → need better biomarkers
Recurrence risk: SGA ~20%; FGR 20–50% (SMFM Consult Series #52, 2020)
2. Classification — Symmetric vs Asymmetric

rows: Decreased cell SIZE (hypertrophy) → catch-up growth possible
Decreased cell NUMBER (fixed deficit) → no catch-up
Small AC; HC/BPD/FL often normal — head-sparing
Neurodevelopment WORSE despite 'brain-sparing' — persistently ↑ CBF = maladaptive (Figueras 2011)
Poor postnatal growth — doesn't respond to nutrition

blocks.text: หมายเหตุสำคัญ: symmetric/asymmetric classification คลินิกใช้ได้ แต่ ไม่ใช่ independent predictor ของ adverse outcome — preterm symmetric vs asymmetric FGR มี similar postnatal growth และ developmental delay.
3. Etiology of Fetal Growth Restriction
3.1 Fetal Etiologies
Chromosomal disorders: account for up to 20% of FGR cases; T13/T18: 50% have FGR; T21, confined placental mosaicism
Structural defects (no chromosomal anomaly): CHD, gastroschisis, urogenital anomalies → ↑ FGR risk
3.2 Maternal and Environmental Etiologies

rows: Adolescent: nutrient competition; advanced maternal age: placental dysfunction
Surrogate for discrimination, resource inequality, healthcare access (Nasiri Am J Epidemiol 2020)
3.5-fold ↑ risk SGA — strongest modifiable risk factor
↓ HC, AC, fetal weight + abnormal UA Doppler (Brar 2021) — ↑ placental vascular resistance
Famine cohorts: <600–900 kcal/day → FGR; low prepregnancy BMI; BUT nutritional suppl. does NOT improve outcomes
Placental insufficiency + malperfusion (most common pregnancy disorder)
Antiphospholipid syndrome → thrombotic vasculopathy → placental infarction
Well-documented: ↓ fetal growth at high altitude vs sea level
~5–10% of FGR; malaria = largest portion; CMV/rubella/toxo/syphilis → cell death + ↓ cell division
Valproic acid, warfarin, cyclophosphamide — timing/dose/metabolism dependent
Maternal HPA axis → ↑ cortisol → fetal insulin resistance + FGR (Valsamakis 2020)

blocks.text: 3.3 Placental Etiologies

Placental Insufficiency — กลไกหลักของ Asymmetric FGR
Incomplete spiral artery remodeling: trophoblast invasion ตื้นเกินไป (shallow invasion) → spiral arteries remain high-resistance vessels → ↓ blood flow + nutrients + O₂ to fetus. ปกติ spiral arteries จะถูก remodel ให้เป็น low-resistance, high-capacity vessels. Mid-to-late pregnancy insults: further reduce placental vascular surface area. Specific disorders: chronic abruption, infarction, circumvallate placenta, chorioangioma, velamentous/marginal cord insertion.

Twin gestation: physical/functional capacity of uterus + placenta exceeded → growth deceleration expected in 3rd trimester; SGA risk ~25% twin, ~60% triplet/quad; monochorionic → unequal placental sharing; TTTS donor = FGR
Note: placenta accreta and previa — NOT consistently associated with FGR
4. Pathophysiology
4.1 Hormonal Axis — Insulin/IGF
Insulin: principal in utero growth factor — cell number (mitogenic), glucose uptake, protein breakdown; FGR: ↓ insulin → ↓ pancreatic beta cells (Limesand & Rozance J Physiol 2017)
Fetal hypoxia → ↑ catecholamines → suppress insulin secretion + sensitivity — key mechanism linking placental insufficiency to metabolic derangements
IGF-1, IGF-2, IGFBP-2, IGFBP-3: implicated in FGR pathophysiology in experimental models
4.2 Placental Vascular Pathophysiology
Disruption of spiral artery remodeling → high-resistance, low-compliance uteroplacental circulation
Hypovascular villi, fibrin deposition, trophoblast degeneration (particularly in preeclampsia-associated FGR)
Fetal compensatory response: ↑ catecholamines → peripheral vasoconstriction → ↑ blood flow to brain and heart → 'brain-sparing'
Brain-sparing is regional, not global: hierarchical blood flow within brain shifts by region during chronic hypoxia — does NOT protect all areas equally
5. Antenatal Screening and Prenatal Management
5.1 Components of Antenatal Screening

rows: Limited by obesity, multiple gestation, fibroids
AC: best stand-alone NPV ~90% for ruling out FGR
Decreases perinatal death ~30% when included in FGR surveillance
Less sensitive in mild placental disease
May predict fetal death, neonatal acidosis, neurologic morbidity
Not routinely recommended; should not dictate care alone

blocks.text: 5.2 Ultrasound — Key Points
AC: best stand-alone specificity for FGR; small AC = small liver = ↓ hepatic glycogen stores + ↓ subcutaneous fat
Serial growth every 3–4 wk: ACOG recommendation — more frequent → ↑ false-positive rate
EFW <10th perc → add: amniotic fluid measurement + anatomy evaluation + Doppler studies
Consider genetic testing / infectious disease workup if: structural anomalies OR early-onset FGR
5.3 UA Doppler — Clinical Use

UA Doppler Prognostication — คลินิก FGR
Absent or reversed EDF: poor prognostic factor; ↑ perinatal mortality; ↓ death ~30% when included in surveillance (Alfirevic Cochrane 2010). UA Doppler less sensitive in mild placental disease — ไม่ใช่ screening tool เดี่ยว. Mechanism: AEDV/REDV occurs when significant portion of placental villous tree is damaged (animal model data). DV/MCA/aortic isthmus: controversial — not routinely recommended as sole guide for delivery decisions (Fanaroff 2025).
6. Delivery Timing
6.1 ACOG Recommendations (Spong 2011)

rows: FGR + oligohydramnios OR abnormal UA Doppler
Early delivery NOT associated with improved outcomes — may be worse

blocks.text: FGR itself does NOT require cesarean delivery — delivery mode based on other obstetric factors
Goal: balance delivery before severe fetal compromise vs consequences of prematurity
Preterm delivery → antenatal corticosteroids + consider MgSO₄; center with MFM + NICU
6.2 Key Trials
DIGITAT (Boers BMJ 2010;341:c7087): n=650; FGR ≥36 wk; induction vs expectant monitoring → no improvement in perinatal survival or long-term outcomes with early delivery
GRIT — Growth Restriction Intervention Trial (Walker Am J Obstet Gynecol 2011;204:34): FGR <34 wk; earlier vs later delivery → long-term outcomes similar; no benefit to early delivery
Between 34–36 wk: no adequately powered RCT exists — individualise based on Doppler + clinical factors
7. Prevention

Low-dose aspirin: recommended for women at high risk for preeclampsia (common cause of FGR) — ACOG. Meta-analysis: aspirin → ↑ birth weight, prolonged gestation, ↓ NICU admission (Bujold Obstet Gynecol 2010;116:402); not yet routinely recommended specifically for FGR prevention (small studies, not conclusive)
Counseling modifiable risk factors: tobacco cessation (3.5x SGA risk), alcohol abstinence, marijuana abstinence, malarial prophylaxis
What does NOT work: nutritional supplementation (protein, vitamins, zinc, calcium, iron, low-salt diet) → NOT effective; bed rest NOT recommended for FGR
Malnutrition only: nutritional correction helps (severe caloric restriction <600–900 kcal/day during famine)
8. Postnatal Evaluation
8.1 Physical Exam — Stigmata of FGR

Asymmetric FGR Neonate — สิ่งที่ต้องประเมินทันทีแรกรับ
Classic features: large wide anterior fontanelle; absent buccal fat; small scaphoid abdomen; thin umbilical cord; ↓ skeletal muscle mass + subcutaneous fat; disproportionately large hands/feet; loose dry skin (nape, axilla, gluteal). Symmetric FGR: เพิ่ม dysmorphic facies (aneuploidies), microcephaly, petechiae, blueberry muffin (CMV), hepatosplenomegaly, murmur, abnormal retinal exam, pancytopenia (congenital infections). Placenta ส่ง pathology: small size, infarctions → ยืนยัน placental insufficiency; TORCH placentitis → guide infectious workup.
8.2 Postnatal Complications Overview

rows: 70% SGA; ↓ glycogen, impaired gluconeogenesis, ↓ fat, ↑ insulin sensitivity, ↑ catecholamines
GIR 6–8 mg/kg/min; formula/tube feed; IV dextrose; diazoxide if hyperinsulinemic
↑ surface area:BW ratio, ↓ subcut fat, ↓ brown fat, ↑ catecholamines
Temp monitor from delivery; double-wall incubator if premature
↓ lung weight + ↓ alveolar number + thickened septa; pulmonary vascular remodeling
Surfactant; iNO for PPHN; SGA 24–26 wk = 2x CLD vs AGA
AEDV/REDV → 4x NEC risk; mesenteric flow not normalized until ~1 wk life
Trophic feeds HM early; delay formula; SMA/celiac velocity monitor
↓ bile acid transport, ↑ PN toxicity sensitivity, altered hepatic metabolism
Minimize PN; early EN; FGR → earlier + more severe cholestasis
Cerebral vascular remodeling, impaired autoregulation, hemodynamic instability
NIRS monitoring; avoid cerebral O₂ fluctuations; cranial US protocol
FGR → higher risk + worse staging than AGA; low IGF-1 + poor postnatal weight gain
Standard ROP screening; earlier first screen
Abnormal innate + humoral immunity → immunodeficient state
Low threshold for sepsis workup; aggressive management

blocks.text: 9. Hypoglycemia and Hypothermia — Detail
9.1 Hypoglycemia
Definition used: <2.6 mmol/L (46 mg/dL); some studies: ~70% SGA infants affected; 1/3 severe, recurrent, or prolonged

Mechanisms: ↓ glycogen stores; impaired gluconeogenesis; ↓ fat mass; sustained ↑ catecholamines; ↑ insulin sensitivity; inability to produce ketone bodies from fatty acids — alternative brain fuel — further exacerbates brain risk during hypoglycemia
Hyperinsulinism subset: chronic hypoxia → pancreatic beta islet hyperplasia → persistent hyperinsulinemia resolving over days–weeks; may need diazoxide short-term
GIR comparison: AGA: 4–6 mg/kg/min; FGR: 6–8 mg/kg/min — higher due to ↑ brain:body weight ratio + ↑ insulin secretion/sensitivity
Serial glucose monitoring: initiate soon after birth; formula/tube feed/IV dextrose bolus + infusion as needed
9.2 Hypothermia
Mechanisms: ↑ surface area:body weight ratio; ↓ subcutaneous fat; ↓ brown adipose tissue deposition in utero; ↑ catecholamines → aggravate metabolic acidemia
Often coincides with hypoglycemia — hypothermia worsens hypoglycemia and vice versa
Management: temperature monitor from delivery; warm delivery room; may need double-walled incubator, especially if preterm FGR
10. Respiratory Complications
10.1 RDS and CLD

FGR + Prematurity = Double Hit สำหรับ Lung
Mechanism paradox: some believe FGR ↑ circulating glucocorticoids → ↑ lung maturity — BUT countered by ↓ lung weight + ↓ alveolar number + thickened septa → poor gas exchange. Net result: ↑ RDS + ↑ CLD. SGA 24–26+6 wk vs AGA same GA: 2x more likely to develop CLD (Sasi Acta Paediatr 2015); FGR preterm → prolonged O₂ support, more likely discharged on supplemental O₂. Long-term: abnormal spirometry + ↓ diffusing capacity → ↑ asthma/COPD risk (Ronkainen Arch Dis Child 2016).
PPHN ใน FGR: pulmonary vascular remodeling + PA endothelial cell dysfunction — iNO response may differ from idiopathic PPHN; Rozance Am J Physiol 2011
10.2 ROP
FGR associated with higher risk + worse staging ROP vs AGA (Chu Sci Rep 2020;10:17591)
Mechanism: CLD + FGR → prolonged O₂ exposure; low IGF-1 + poor postnatal weight gain → ↑ ROP progression
Screening: standard protocol; consider earlier first exam if very low IGF-1 or very poor growth velocity
11. NEC and Feeding in FGR

AEDV/REDV → NEC 4x — กลไกและ Feeding Strategy
Mechanism: chronic fetal hypoxemia → poor in utero mesenteric perfusion → intestinal + mucosal injury before birth. After birth: superior mesenteric + celiac artery flow velocity ไม่ normalize จนกว่า ~1 สัปดาห์ชีวิต (Maruyama J Perinat Med 2001). เมื่อ metabolic demands ของ enteral feeding + gut hypoperfusion + dysbiosis + stasis + bacterial translocation → NEC. Meta-analysis: neonates with AEDV/REDV: 4x more likely NEC vs forward EDF (Dorling ADC 2005). Strategy: trophic/minimal enteral nutrition ด้วย human milk early; delay formula; most studies show no harm from early trophic HM (Leaf Pediatrics 2012).
11.2 PNALD ใน FGR
FGR → cholestasis earlier + more severe + more prolonged vs AGA (Robinson & Ehrenkranz J Pediatr 2008;152:59)
Mechanisms: ↓ bile acid transport, ↑ sensitivity PN toxicity, impaired hepatic fatty acid + glucose metabolism, ↓ antioxidant capacity
FGR neonates ↓ capacity to use parenteral protein in first week of life → energy + protein overload risk (Boehm Acta Paediatr 1988)
Management: minimize PN duration; advance EN as early as feasible with HM; careful PN dose titration
DOHaD link: FGR → PNALD → may amplify later NAFLD risk — connection under investigation
12. Intracranial Hemorrhage, HIE, and Neurodevelopment
12.1 Brain Injury in FGR

ICH risk: cerebral vascular remodeling, impaired cerebral autoregulation, hemodynamic instability — mechanism debated; some studies: MCA Doppler ↑ associated with ICH; others not after confounder correction

NIRS monitoring: serial cerebral oxygenation measurements → help predict neurodevelopment; avoid fluctuations in cerebral O₂ (Noori J Pediatr 2014); NIRS better than BP values — BP ≠ tissue perfusion in NICU

HIE: FGR → fetal intolerance to labor → fetal hypoxemia + acidemia → low Apgar; placental insufficiency (thrombotic vasculopathies) plays key role in HIE; 5–30x ↑ morbidity/mortality vs AGA even at term
PVL: ↑ risk in FGR preterm; periventricular leukomalacia (Malhotra Front Endocrinol 2019)
12.2 Neurodevelopmental Outcomes

Brain-Sparing ≠ Brain Protection — Paradox ที่ต้องเข้าใจ
Studies (Figueras Ultrasound OG 2011;38:288): neurodevelopmental outcomes WORSE in asymmetric FGR (brain-sparing) than expected. Mechanism: persistently ↑ cerebral blood flow after birth = maladaptive (loss of cerebral vasoreactivity) → relative hyperoxia + reperfusion injury. Cellular: arrest in subventricular neural progenitor cells → ↓ cortical volumes. MRI: ↓ brain volume, ↓ gray matter, ↓ hippocampal volumes → persist into childhood → ↓ cognition, attention, mood, social skills; ↑ risk CP + neuropsychiatric disorders.
13. Long-Term Consequences — DOHaD
13.1 Developmental Origins of Health and Disease

DOHaD — Barker Hypothesis และความหมายต่อ NICU Care
Barker DJ Trends Endocrinol Metab 2002: fetal adaptations during starvation/undernourishment in utero = 'thrifty phenotype'. After birth: exposed to abundance → mismatch between in utero vs postnatal environment → insulin resistance, obesity, CVD. Mechanism: epigenetic programming — in utero environment → gene expression → phenotype. แหล่งหลัก: epidemiologic studies ผ่าน Dutch Hunger Winter (WWII famine) cohort — สตรีที่อดอาหารช่วงตั้งครรภ์ → ลูกโตแล้วมี metabolic syndrome สูง.
Cardiometabolic sequelae: insulin resistance/T2DM; dyslipidemia; CVD; hypertension; obesity; NAFLD; renal insufficiency
Neuropsychiatric: schizophrenia, depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, Alzheimer disease
Reproductive: premature pubarche, PCOS; shorter life span; immune dysfunction
Cancer: some epidemiologic associations

Catch-up growth trade-off: optimized postnatal growth → better neurocognitive outcomes; BUT rapid weight gain first 2 years → ↑ cardiometabolic syndrome risk — balance ดูแล growth velocity, ไม่ให้ overfeed ให้ catch-up เร็วเกินไป
14. NICU Practical — เตรียมรับ FGR Neonate
ก่อนคลอด: รับ history จาก OB: UA Doppler result (AEDV/REDV?); GA; chorionicity (twin); fetal anomaly; antenatal steroids given?
Delivery room: skilled team; warm resuscitation; FGR อดทนต่อ labor ไม่ดี — fetal intolerance to labor → low Apgar, HIE risk; prepare for intubation
Glucose: serial glucose monitoring ทันทีหลังคลอด; target >2.6 mmol/L (46 mg/dL); GIR 6–8 mg/kg/min; ถ้า hyperinsulinemic: diazoxide
Temperature: double-wall incubator สำหรับ preterm FGR; ห้อง warm; servo-controlled skin probe; hypothermia + hypoglycemia cycle ต้องตัดวงจรทั้งคู่พร้อมกัน
Feeding strategy (AEDV/REDV): trophic HM feeds ≤20 ml/kg/day ระยะแรก; ไม่รีบ advance; monitor mesenteric flow; avoid formula early; SMA velocity check ก่อน advance feeds ถ้ามี POCUS
PN: start PN เพื่อ support แต่ระวัง overload protein Week 1; advance amino acids cautiously; PNALD เกิดเร็วและรุนแรงกว่า AGA
Polycythemia: check venous Hct Day 1–2; >65% + symptomatic → partial exchange transfusion
Cranial US: NIRS ถ้ามี; cranial US Day 7/28/36–40 wk CGA (<30 wk); avoid BP fluctuation — ไม่ใช่ surrogate ของ brain perfusion ใน FGR
ROP: standard ROP screening; watch weight gain + IGF-1 trend
Growth: target appropriate catch-up growth — avoid rapid weight gain (DOHaD risk) แต่ undernutrition = ↓ neurocognitive outcome; head circumference gain > weight gain = priority first
Genetics/Infectious workup: early FGR + structural anomaly → karyotype/CMA; symmetric FGR + microcephaly/rash/HSM → TORCH PCR + ophthalmology + audiology
Discharge planning: โน้มนำ family: FGR ไม่จบที่ NICU — ติดตาม neurodevelopment ยาว; แจ้ง cardiometabolic risk ใน adulthood (DOHaD)
15. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — FGR + AEDV/REDV: NEC 4x Risk → Trophic HM Only, No Formula Rush
AEDV/REDV → mesenteric perfusion ไม่ normalize จนกว่า 1 wk ชีวิต. ให้ trophic HM feeds (≤20 ml/kg/day) และ hold formula. Meta-analysis: neonates AEDV/REDV = 4x NEC (Dorling ADC 2005). ดูจาก prenatal Doppler ก่อน set feeding plan — ไม่ใช่แค่ GA/BW อย่างเดียว.

PEARL 2 — GIR 6–8 ไม่ใช่ 4–6 ใน FGR: Brain:Body Ratio + Hyperinsulinism
AGA: GIR 4–6 mg/kg/min เพียงพอ. FGR: GIR 6–8 เพราะ (1) ↑ brain:body weight ratio = สัดส่วน brain glucose demand ต่อ body weight สูงกว่า (2) ↑ insulin sensitivity + ↑ catecholamine-suppressed insulin secretion. Hyperinsulinemic subset (chronic hypoxia → islet hyperplasia): diazoxide short-term. หลีกเลี่ยง hypoglycemia <2.6 mmol/L = seizures + brain injury.

PEARL 3 — Brain-Sparing ≠ Brain Safety: Neurodevelopment WORSE ใน Asymmetric FGR
Paradox: asymmetric FGR (70–80%) มี head-sparing — HC > weight percentile. แต่ Figueras 2011: neurodevelopmental outcomes WORSE ใน asymmetric vs symmetric FGR. Mechanism: persistent ↑ CBF postnatally = loss of vasoreactivity → relative hyperoxia + reperfusion. NIRS ≠ blood pressure — BP ไม่ใช่ surrogate ของ brain perfusion ใน FGR. Monitor cerebral O₂ ด้วย NIRS ถ้าทำได้.

PEARL 4 — DOHaD Trade-off: Catch-Up Growth ต้องช้าและระมัดระวัง
เป้าหมาย: neurocognitive recovery ต้องการ optimized growth — แต่ rapid catch-up weight first 2 yr → ↑ insulin resistance/obesity/CVD (DOHaD). ดังนั้น: target head circumference gain > weight gain ระยะแรก; avoid aggressive formula overfeeding หลัง discharge; inform family ว่า FGR neonate มี long-term cardiometabolic risk — track เป็น high-risk patient ตลอด childhood.

PEARL 5 — Symmetric FGR + Dysmorphic/Microcephaly → Workup ก่อน Assume Placental Cause
Symmetric FGR (20–30%) = early insult → chromosomal (T13/T18/T21 account for up to 20% FGR) หรือ TORCH. T13/T18: 50% มี FGR. ดังนั้น: symmetric + microcephaly → TORCH PCR + ophthalmology; dysmorphic → karyotype/CMA ก่อน assume 'placentally small'. Placental pathology ส่ง routine — ถ้า pathology ไม่ match placental insufficiency pattern → expand workup. ไม่ assume asymmetric = placental, symmetric = genetic — overlap มีเสมอ.
16. Evidence Update — Key Studies
Flenady V et al Lancet 2011;377(9774):1331: meta-analysis 96 studies; SGA attributable risk for stillbirth ~23%; FGR = leading cause perinatal morbidity/mortality after prematurity
SMFM Consult Series #52 Am J Obstet Gynecol 2020;223:B2: diagnosis and management FGR; recurrence SGA 20%, FGR 20–50%; UA Doppler ↓ perinatal death ~30%; delivery timing recommendations
Boers KE et al BMJ 2010;341:c7087 (DIGITAT): FGR ≥36 wk; induction vs expectant → no improvement in perinatal survival or long-term outcomes with earlier delivery
Walker DM et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2011;204:34 (GRIT long-term): FGR <34 wk; earlier vs later delivery → long-term outcomes similar; no benefit to early delivery
Bujold E et al Obstet Gynecol 2010;116:402: aspirin meta-analysis: ↑ birth weight, prolonged gestation, ↓ NICU admission in at-risk pregnancies
Sasi A et al Acta Paediatr 2015;104:e552 (IUGR + preterm lung): SGA 24–26+6 wk = 2x more likely CLD vs AGA; FGR preterm → prolonged O₂ support, home O₂
Ronkainen E et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2016;101:F412: IUGR + very preterm → lower lung function at school age; ↑ asthma/COPD risk
Dorling J et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2005;90:F359: AEDV/REDV meta-analysis: 4x more likely NEC vs forward EDF — key evidence for cautious feeding strategy
Leaf A et al Pediatrics 2012;129:e1260: early vs delayed enteral feeding FGR preterm: early trophic HM not harmful; optimal timing for AEDV/REDV still unknown
Robinson DT & Ehrenkranz RA J Pediatr 2008;152:59: PNALD SGA: earlier + more severe + prolonged cholestasis; ↑ risk after correcting for PN days + GA
Figueras F et al Ultrasound OG 2011;38:288: neurobehavioral outcomes preterm FGR: WORSE in those with advanced brain-sparing signs prenatal; paradox of 'protective brain-sparing'
Noori S et al J Pediatr 2014;164:264: cerebral blood flow changes in relation to peri/IVH in extremely preterm: NIRS better than BP for monitoring brain perfusion
Limesand SW & Rozance PJ J Physiol 2017;595:5103: fetal insulin secretion adaptations in placental insufficiency: ↑ catecholamines suppress insulin → beta cell reduction — key pathophysiology
Barker DJ Trends Endocrinol Metab 2002;13:364: fetal programming coronary heart disease — DOHaD foundational; 'thrifty phenotype hypothesis'
Chu A et al Sci Rep 2020;10:17591: prenatal IUGR + ROP risk: FGR associated with higher risk and worse staging; low IGF-1 role
Brar BK et al J Matern Fetal Neonatal Med 2021;34:3330: marijuana exposure: ↓ fetal HC, AC, weight + abnormal UA Doppler — independent of tobacco use
Malhotra A et al Front Endocrinol 2019;10:55: neonatal morbidities of FGR: comprehensive review of pathophysiology and impact of short/long-term complications
Maruyama K & Koizumi T J Perinat Med 2001;29:64: SMA blood flow velocity SGA VLBW: ไม่ normalize จนกว่า ~1 สัปดาห์ชีวิต — mechanism underlying NEC risk
17. Self-Check Q&A
Q: FGR กับ SGA ต่างกันอย่างไร? ทำไม communication ระหว่าง OB และ neonatologist จึงสำคัญ?

ตอบ: FGR: fetus ไม่ถึง growth potential เพราะ pathologic insult — may be born at any percentile. SGA: BW <10th perc — most constitutionally small (false-positive rate สูง). Communication OB–Neo จำเป็น เพื่อรู้ว่า SGA infant มี true growth deceleration, Doppler abnormality, หรือ placental pathology จริงหรือไม่ — เปลี่ยน management ทั้งหมด.
Q: Asymmetric vs symmetric FGR: สัดส่วน, etiology, postnatal growth ต่างกันอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Asymmetric: 70–80%, late onset, placental insufficiency, ↓ cell SIZE → catch-up growth possible. Symmetric: 20–30%, early onset, chromosomal/TORCH/severe maternal, ↓ cell NUMBER → fixed deficit, no catch-up. แต่ outcome neurodevelopmental WORSE ใน asymmetric (brain-sparing paradox; Figueras 2011).
Q: ทำไม FGR neonate ต้องการ GIR 6–8 mg/kg/min แทน 4–6?

ตอบ: เพราะ (1) ↑ brain:body weight ratio → ↑ proportional brain glucose demand; (2) ↑ insulin sensitivity + impaired gluconeogenesis + ↓ glycogen/fat stores; (3) subset มี hyperinsulinism (chronic hypoxia → beta islet hyperplasia) → prolonged hypoglycemia → diazoxide if persistent.
Q: NEC risk ใน FGR + AEDV/REDV เป็นกี่เท่า และ feeding strategy ควรเป็นอย่างไร?

ตอบ: 4x more likely NEC vs forward EDF (Dorling ADC 2005). Mechanism: mesenteric flow ไม่ normalize จนกว่า ~1 wk ชีวิต. Strategy: trophic human milk feeds ≤20 ml/kg/day; ไม่ advance เร็ว; หลีกเลี่ยง formula ระยะแรก (Leaf Pediatrics 2012).
Q: ACOG แนะนำ delivery timing ใน isolated FGR vs FGR ที่มี oligohydramnios/abnormal UA Doppler?

ตอบ: Isolated FGR: 38 0/7 – 39 6/7 wk. FGR + oligohydramnios หรือ abnormal UA Doppler: 34 0/7 – 37 0/7 wk. DIGITAT/GRIT: no benefit to early delivery for ≥36 wk or <34 wk FGR. FGR + aneuploidy/infection: early delivery → worse outcomes.
Q: Brain-sparing ใน FGR คืออะไร และทำไม paradoxically neurodevelopment แย่ลง?

ตอบ: Brain-sparing: placental insufficiency → peripheral vasoconstriction + cerebral vasodilation → preferential cardiac output to brain (MCA-PI ลด). แต่ persistent ↑ CBF after birth = maladaptive — loss of cerebral vasoreactivity → relative hyperoxia + reperfusion injury (Figueras 2011). ↓ brain volume, gray matter, hippocampal volumes persist into childhood. Brain-sparing ≠ brain protection.
Q: DOHaD คืออะไร และเกี่ยวข้องกับ FGR neonate อย่างไร?

ตอบ: DOHaD (Developmental Origins of Health and Disease): fetal adaptations during in utero undernutrition/hypoxia → epigenetic programming → after birth, abundance environment → mismatch → ↑ insulin resistance/obesity/T2DM/CVD/HTN/NAFLD ใน adulthood (Barker 2002). Catch-up growth trade-off: rapid weight gain first 2 yr → ↑ cardiometabolic risk; แต่ undernutrition → ↓ neurocognition. NICU เป็นจุดเริ่มต้น set trajectory ชีวิต.
Q: Symmetric FGR ที่มี microcephaly + petechiae: ควร workup อะไรบ้าง?

ตอบ: Symmetric FGR + microcephaly + petechiae → congenital infection (TORCH): CMV, rubella, toxoplasma, syphilis, HIV. Workup: TORCH PCR (urine CMV PCR ที่แม่นที่สุดสำหรับ cCMV ภายใน 3 สัปดาห์); ophthalmology; audiology; karyotype/CMA (aneuploidies: T13/T18/T21 = up to 20% FGR cases); LFT + CBC (HSM + pancytopenia); brain MRI/cranial US.
Q: PNALD ใน FGR: เหตุใดจึงเกิดเร็วและรุนแรงกว่า AGA?

ตอบ: FGR: ↓ bile acid transport; ↑ PN toxicity sensitivity; ↓ antioxidant capacity; impaired hepatic fatty acid + glucose metabolism; ↓ capacity use parenteral protein Week 1 (Boehm 1988). Robinson & Ehrenkranz 2008: FGR → cholestasis earlier + more severe + prolonged after correcting for PN days + GA. Management: minimize PN; advance HM early; careful protein titration.
Q: Low-dose aspirin ใน FGR prevention: evidence อยู่ที่ไหน?

ตอบ: Bujold Obstet Gynecol 2010;116:402: aspirin started early pregnancy: ↑ birth weight, ↑ gestation, ↓ NICU admission in at-risk group. แต่ ยังไม่ recommended routinely specifically for FGR prevention (small studies). ACOG แนะนำ low-dose aspirin สำหรับ high-risk preeclampsia (preeclampsia = common cause FGR). Nutritional supplements, bed rest: NOT effective for FGR prevention.

Day 112 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.15
บทต่อไป: Chapter 15 — Developmental Origins of Health and Disease (DOHaD). เตรียมตัว: epigenetic mechanisms (DNA methylation, histone modification, miRNA); Barker hypothesis in depth; Dutch Hunger Winter cohort; maternal obesity + FGR + DOHaD; postnatal nutrition strategy (catch-up growth timing); specific organ programming (cardiovascular, metabolic, neuro, renal); implications for NICU nutritional practice.
